tool.name: aligncopy
tool.summary: Copy a single alignment unchanged through the shared alignment IO path

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `aligncopy` is the alignment-preserving equivalent of `seqret` for the current Epithema alignment surface. It loads one aligned FASTA or Stockholm alignment through the shared alignment reader and writes the same alignment payload back through the governed alignment result path without modifying rows, columns, or metadata.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current v1 interface accepts exactly one local alignment input path. Supported input formats are the alignment formats already handled by the shared alignment loader, currently aligned FASTA and Stockholm.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The result is the normalized alignment payload itself. Through the CLI, Epithema renders alignment outputs as Stockholm by default so the copied alignment remains structured and round-trippable.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema aligncopy`. The declared example below matches the existing Rust service test path that loads a committed multiple-alignment fixture and confirms row-count and alignment-identifier preservation.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release is intentionally narrow: `aligncopy` is a normalization and pass-through command, not an editing or reformatting workflow. Historical EMBOSS option breadth is not reproduced yet.

artifacts.label: Multiple-alignment Stockholm fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed Stockholm alignment fixture with three aligned rows and a stable alignment identifier.

examples.title: Copy a multiple Stockholm alignment unchanged
examples.description: Loads the committed three-row Stockholm alignment fixture and returns the same alignment through the governed output path.

expected_outputs.label: Copied alignment payload
expected_outputs.description: A three-row alignment that preserves the input alignment identifier and column layout.
